01 The Goal - YouTube.mp4
Welcome to the Scalable OS Accelerator. I am so glad you're here. You know, our purpose at The Scalable Company is to help entrepreneurs scale themselves so that they can scale their businesses. We fundamentally believe that companies are better, teams are better, families are better, and whole world economies are better when founding entrepreneurs stay at the helm of their companies as long as they want to. And that's exactly what this program is all about. The goal of this accelerator is to help you build and install a business operating system so that company can run and scale without you. In other words, we want you to put systems in place so that your business can grow without you having to sacrifice your health, your family, and everything that you hold dear at the altar of scale.
But why is this a worthy goal? Why should you seek to install an operating system so that your business can run and scale without you? Well, please allow me to answer that question with another question. Do you run your business or does your business run you? Let me ask it even more bluntly. Do you own your business or does your business own you? You know, for most of us, we started our businesses because we wanted more money. More freedom, to make more of an impact. In other words, we started our businesses because we wanted more. More for ourselves, more for the people around us. More for the world at large. But somewhere along the way, it became less about what you needed and more about what your business needed. Am I right?
Somewhere along the way, things just stopped working like they used to. If this sounds familiar, I want to encourage you. This is not your fault. In fact, if any of this is ringing true, it's only because you have experienced a fair bit of success. And in many ways, you have become a victim of that success. So the good news is, if you're feeling this frustration right now, it's a sign that you're doing something right. The bad news is, however, what 'What' got you here won't get you where you want to go. But the great news is that you are in the right place. You see, we understand the problem that you are dealing with and we know how to fix it.
The problem is most businesses are running on what we refer to as a UOS instead of a scalable OS. With a UOS. You are the operating system and the operating system is you. Now, to be clear, a 'you' operating system was fine when your business was small. Maybe you were only selling a single product or serving just one type of client. Your team, assuming you even had a team back then, could literally fit around the same table. And share a single pepperoni pizza for lunch. Things weren't always easy, but they were simple and they were pretty fun. And most importantly, you could manage it all because, Well, frankly, there just wasn't that much to manage. But as businesses grow, a single operating system is no longer enough.
They're just—n't enough mass in any one person to keep a complex and growing company in orbit. And before too long, the business starts flinging you around. Like a ragdoll. And just like your computer will grind to a halt when your operating system is old or corrupt, your business will grind to a halt if your company operating system is corrupt or out of date. The result? Companies stall out and founders burn out. Fortunately, the solution to this challenge is simple. You need to replace your operating system with a scalable operating system that scales as your business scales. I want you to write this down because this is really, really important. Companies exist to fulfill the needs of its customers and its shareholders. Period. In other words, it is time for your business to serve you, not the other way around. Because let's face it, when you are fulfilled, you'll be able to give even more to your customers, more to your team, and more to your mission. And that's exactly what you need to achieve your next level of scale. So... That's our purpose, that's our goal, and that's what we're all about. I don't know about you, but I am excited to get started. So let's do just that.
